CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2018 Continued To Sep-05-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Conditional Certification, Approval Of Class Notice, Setting Of Final Approval Hearing Date
Off Calendar Apr-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jill Vecchi, Esq. of The Turley & Mara law Firm, T:619.234.2833, for plaintiff/moving party;
Jason E. Shapiro, Esq. of Littler Mendelson PC, T:415.439.6264, for defendant.

RULING: There being no opposition or objection, and to the satisfacton of the Court,

PLAINTIFF DARYL BELTON's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Conditional Certification, Approval Of Class Notice, Setting Of Final Approval Hearing Date is GRANTED. A Final Approval Hearing is set for July 26, 2018 at 9:30am in Department 302.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

Notice Of Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Conditional Certification, Approval Of Class Notice, Setting Of Final Approval Hearing Date
Off Calendar Apr-05-2018 Continued To Apr-12-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JILL VECCHI, ESQ. of The Turley & Maria Law Firm (Tel: 619-234-2833) appearing for the plaintiff;
Atty. JASON E. SHAPIRO, ESQ. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 415-439-6264) appearing for defebdabt Braden Partners, L.P.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

The matter is discussed by the Court and counsel for parties. The Court indicates that the parties have made significant progress regarding the Kullar analysis. The Court indicates that it approves the class portion of the motion. However, the Court indicates that there is still a problem as to the PAGA claim and that the court is troubled by the $25,000 amount. The Court indicates that the parties need to provide a rationale that accords with the general policies identified in the most recent PAGA decisions for both a quantitative and qualitative analysis. The Court recommends that the parties re-read the most recent decision by Judge Chen regarding the Uber case.

The Court continues the matter to April 12, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion, for further hearing on plaintiff's unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Conditional Certification, Approval Of Class Notice, Setting Of Final Approval Hearing Date. Plaintiff is to file a supplemental brief (which defendant should participate on) by April 6, 2018, and deliver a courtesy copy directly to Dept. 302. The Court indicates that the supplemental briefing need only address the PAGA claim portion of the motion.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (302/HK)

Notice Of Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Conditional Certification, Approval Of Class Notice, Setting Of Final Approval Hearing Date
